task_completion.description: Verify that a patient evaluation or encounter is opened for patient Luna with attending physician Cameron Cordara.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GNU Health interface showing an evaluation/encounter record with 'Luna' in the patient field and 'Cordara' (or Cameron Cordara) in the health professional/physician field.

task_completion.description: Verify that the urgency level of the presentation is documented.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Urgency', 'Triage', or similar field is filled out indicating an acute, severe, or emergency state.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required vital signs (fever and tachycardia) are recorded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Vital signs section showing a Temperature of 38.0 C or higher, and a Heart Rate of 100 bpm or higher.

task_completion.description: Verify that an appropriate ICD-10 diagnosis code for the abdominal pain/appendicitis is recorded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Diagnosis or Assessment section showing an ICD-10 code starting with 'K' (ideally between K35 and K38 for appendicitis).

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two relevant laboratory tests are ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Laboratory test orders section or tab showing at least 2 distinct tests requested (e.g., CBC, CRP, Urinalysis).

task_completion.description: Verify that a determination on surgical consultation is documented.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A follow-up, consultation, or referral section showing a surgical consultation or surgical follow-up scheduled within 7 days.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client interface with standard window decorations, menus, and forms, rather than a terminal window executing SQL or Python scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is entered into the specific structured fields of the GNU Health application (e.g., dropdowns, specific text boxes for vitals), not a generic text editor like gedit, nano, or a basic notepad.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is actually interacting with a patient record form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GNU Health interface shows a new or saved record tab (e.g., 'Patient Evaluation' or 'Encounter') being actively filled out, rather than just a search screen or static text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient name is 'Luna' (no last name). Attending physician is 'Cameron Cordara'. Vital signs must show Temperature >= 38.0 °C and Heart Rate >= 100 bpm. At least 2 laboratory tests must be ordered. The diagnosis must use an ICD-10 code starting with 'K' (e.g., K35-K38 for appendicitis). A surgical consultation/follow-up must be scheduled within 7 days.
